extraction.one_line_summary: ARU Fabio attacked in a massive gear at 1.5 miles to go on La Planche des Belles Filles, soloing to his first Tour stage win while FROOME took yellow.
tactical_patterns: Team Sky deployed six riders to base of final climb, unprecedented depth for 5.9km finish suggests insurance against Froome off-form.
Aru attacked in oversized gear initially appearing doomed, but sustained power on steep gradient dropped everyone despite tactical inefficiency.
Dan Martin counterattacked seconds after Aru, exploiting Port's hesitation to gain time while Froome watched—revealing GC hierarchy uncertainty.
Yates offensive riding secured white jersey by minimal gaps, suggesting 2017 youth competition unusually tight (seconds not hours) unlike historical pattern.

rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: won yellow but lacked expected emphatic dominance on first mountain test, suggesting less sharpness than previous Tours despite fresh preparation claims
rider_signals.ARU Fabio: positive: two wins in two weeks indicates peaking form trajectory, looks exceptionally light on steep gradients unlike typical Grand Tour fade pattern
rider_signals.PORT Richie: negative: locked up when Martin counterattacked, losing additional time through hesitation not climbing legs—continues chronic trend of losing seconds to Froome
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: timed counterattack precisely after Aru initial surge, gaining time on Port while Froome neutralized—tactically sharper than positioning suggested
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: best-looking climber in GC group per commentary, offensive riding unusual for white jersey contender prioritizing defense
